---
title: "sms spam"
output: html_document
date: "2025-10-08"
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytext)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(textstem)
library(tm)
library(wordcloud)
```
# Data cleaning
```{r}
sms_data <- read.csv("spam.csv")
dim(sms_data)
head(sms_data)

# Remove duplicates
sms_data <- sms_data[!duplicated(sms_data), ]
sum(is.na(sms_data)) # 0 NA data

sms_data <- sms_data %>% 
  select(v2,v1) %>%
  rename(text = v2, label = v1) %>%
  mutate(label = factor(label, levels = c("ham", "spam"), labels = c(0,1)))


data<- sms_data %>%
  mutate(text = text %>% 
           str_replace_all("[^A-Za-z@]", " ") %>%
           str_squish() %>%
           str_to_lower() %>%
           str_replace_all("@", "twacc")) %>% # emails will use @
  mutate(text= lemmatize_strings(text)) %>%
  mutate(text = removeWords(text, stopwords("en")))
head(data)
dim(data)
```

# Data set distribution of classes
```{r}
ggplot(data=data, aes(x=label, fill=label)) + geom_bar()
# Massive difference in no. of rows of both labels.
```

# Word cloud of both labels
```{r}

# Plotting word cloud for ham and spam data 

ham_data <- data[data$label == 0,]
spam_data <- data[data$label == 1,]

ham_words_freq <- termFreq(ham_data$text)
spam_words_freq <- termFreq(spam_data$text)


# Ham word cloud
wordcloud(words = names(ham_words_freq), 
          freq = ham_words_freq, 
          max.words = 100,
          min.freq = 0,
          random.order = FALSE,
          rot.per = 0.35)
        

# Spam word cloud
wordcloud(words = names(spam_words_freq), 
          freq = spam_words_freq, 
          max.words = 100,
          min.freq = 0,
          random.order = FALSE,
          rot.per = 0.35)


```
# Comparing frequency of most common words in both labels
```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidytext)

top_words <- data %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, text) %>%
  count(label, word, sort = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(label) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 30) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(word = reorder_within(word, n, label))

ggplot(top_words, aes(x = n, y = word, fill = factor(label))) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ label, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_reordered() +
  labs(
    title = "30 most common Words in each label",
    x = "Frequency",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))

# There is quite a bit of overlap, such as 'u', 'call', 'now', 'get', 'just'. Not as much overlap as wanted, but at least that means the word-frequency alone isnt rly enough to predict the labels. 
```

# Creating the Document Term Matrix
```{r}
corpus <- VCorpus(VectorSource(data$text))
DTM <- DocumentTermMatrix(corpus)
dim(DTM) # 6106 unique words
words_freq <- termFreq(data$text)

frequent_words <- words_freq[words_freq>=10]

wordcloud(words = names(frequent_words), freq = frequent_words, min.freq=0, max.words=100, random.order=FALSE, rot.per=0.35)

DTM <- DTM[,names(frequent_words)]

dim(DTM) # 706 unique words

# dataframe of DTM 
DTM_df <- DTM %>% as.matrix %>% as_tibble

```
